ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a kimchi stew (jjigae) class; no specific chicken kimchi jjigae subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 has a kimchi jjigae entry; no chicken-specific variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for chicken kimchi soup.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of chicken kimchi soup is approximately 400 g, equivalent to one restaurant bowl as typically served in Korean eateries in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Kimchi Jjigae (Kimchi Stew) — adapted with chicken protein values from USDA FoodData Central; kimchi stew base from National Institute of Agricultural Sciences (NAS) Korea Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.organization: National Institute of Agricultural Sciences (NAS), Rural Development Administration, Republic of Korea; USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from the Korean NAS Food Composition Database entry for kimchi jjigae (pork-based) and adjusted for chicken by substituting pork protein/fat values with USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken breast values. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Sodium reflects typical kimchi stew sodium content from NAS data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed Korean stew dishes — analogous estimate based on kimchi jjigae GI data; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for chicken kimchi soup exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 based on kimchi jjigae analogous entries; kimchi-based stews are low-GI due to high water content, fermented vegetables, and modest carbohydrate load. Carbohydrate per serving = 16.8 g (4.2 g/100g × 400 g serving). GL = (35 × 16.8) / 100 = 5.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from kimchi and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sodium preparation, as a 400 g serving provides approximately 1920 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken kimchi soup has a low estimated GI of 35 and a glycemic load of approximately 5.9 per serving, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added rice or noodles.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat, offset by lean chicken protein and fermented vegetable benefits
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the lean chicken and fermented kimchi provide beneficial protein and probiotics, the high sodium content (~1920 mg per serving) is a concern for cardiovascular disease management and patients should monitor cumulative daily sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Kimchi Soup (닭고기 김치찌개, Dakgogi Kimchi Jjigae) is a Korean stew made by simmering fermented kimchi with chicken pieces, tofu, garlic, gochugaru (Korean red chilli flakes), soy sauce, and sesame oil in a seasoned broth. It is a variation of the classic kimchi jjigae, which traditionally uses pork, adapted here with chicken for a leaner protein profile. The dish is characterised by a bold, spicy, sour, and umami-rich flavour derived from well-fermented kimchi, and a hearty, warming texture with tender chicken and soft tofu. It is typically served in a stone pot or ceramic bowl alongside steamed white rice and banchan (Korean side dishes). Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 62 kcal, 5.8 g protein, 2.4 g fat, and 4.2 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of 480 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management when consumed without high-GI accompaniments. Clinically, the primary concern is sodium load from kimchi and soy sauce, which is relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The fermented kimchi contributes dietary fibre, vitamin C, and probiotic cultures. In Singapore, this dish is served at Korean restaurants and bibimbap specialty outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kimchi jjigae with chicken?
Is kimchi soup good for diabetics?
Is chicken kimchi soup high in sodium?
Can I eat kimchi soup if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of kimchi soup?
meal_context: Served with steamed rice and banchan

confidence_reason: No exact entry for chicken kimchi soup was found in a single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from the Korean NAS Food Composition Database kimchi jjigae entry with chicken substitution using USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database kimchi stew data. Medium confidence is appropriate for adapted multi-source estimates.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the Korean NAS Food Composition Database for kimchi jjigae, and the USDA SR Legacy chicken entry does not provide a reliable value for this composite dish. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this dish type. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the Korean NAS source for kimchi jjigae. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible in this dish type per NAS data. Sodium is high at 480 mg/100 g (approximately 1920 mg per 400 g serving) due to fermented kimchi and soy sauce; this is the primary clinical concern. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous kimchi jjigae entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. GL per serving = (35 × 16.8) / 100 = 5.9. Halal status is not confirmed as kimchi may contain fish sauce or shrimp paste depending on preparation. Gluten-free status is not confirmed due to soy sauce use. The food_id GD003916 is preserved from the field survey data seed record.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutrient profile from Korean NAS Food Composition Database (kimchi jjigae) adapted with USDA SR Legacy chicken values.
Estimated GI at 35 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous kimchi stew entries with full documentation.
Added Korean local name 닭고기 김치찌개 and romanized Dakgogi Kimchi Jjigae to satisfy G6 Korean cuisine requirement.
Added Chinese simplified 鸡肉泡菜汤 and traditional 雞肉泡菜湯 from input data.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source justification in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Set all ontology match_type values to allowed set only (exact, closest_parent, none).
Confirmed gi_category='low' for GI=35 per ≤55 threshold rule.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with machine key type2_diabetes and human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
